Summary

  • A high-class, steady batter — strong vs spin, tight technique.
  • Stronger against spin (57) than pace (32); pace — especially the quicks — is the more productive line of attack.
  • They get out most often to balls that are full and on the stumps (3.1 dismissals/100).
  • Most often out bowled (27% of dismissals); most often to right fast (28).

Common themes

  • right-hand bat, averaging 44.3 at a strike rate of 50.4.
  • Carries a big share of the batting — 16.1% of team runs.
  • Scores mainly through the off side (41%).

Strengths

  • Main scoring shot is the work/nudge (37% of runs).
  • The sweep brings them 16% of their runs — 2.3× the typical Test batter's share.
  • Stronger against spin (57) than pace (32); pace — especially the quicks — is the more productive line of attack.

Weaknesses / how to get him

  • Somewhat more vulnerable early (1.4 vs 1.0 dismissals/100 once set). Early runs come mainly off the work/nudge (38%).
  • Most often out bowled (27% of dismissals); most often to right fast (28).
  • Vulnerable to the good length (averages 33 there vs 44 overall; 0.9 dismissals/100).
  • Struggles on the stumps (21 avg, 2.4 outs/100).
  • The ball that seams in hurts them (avg 10 vs 48 when it holds its line).
  • Most dangerous ball: full and on the stumps (3.1 dismissals/100, avg 27).
  • His cut is high-risk (false shot 34%, 0.0 outs/100).

Match Impact (share of runs)

Makes 16.1% of their team's runs over a career (typical innings 13.3%), and 4.2% of all runs in their matches; carries the innings (≥25% of the team's runs) 14% of the time, and dominates (≥40%) 3%.

Suggested Fields (the stock field ± their evidenced deviations)

Each field starts from the stock template for that bowler type & phase (orthodox because it works), then makes at most three changes — each one earned by a trigger in their game clearing the cohort P75 bar (do they lap, cut, pull, score square, edge early?). Change rows are highlighted with their stat; Stock rows carry the orthodoxy line. The read line backtests the changes against the pure stock field. Early = first 30 balls; Set = once in. Drawn from behind the bowler (bowler bottom, striker top); off side is on the left.

Bouncer plan
A standard bumper field — he isn't a heavy puller (P28), so this is a holding / surprise option, not a wicket-taking plan. One slip, a catcher in front of square, and the two legal leg-side riders (deep backward square + deep fine leg).
FielderStock/ChangeWhy they're there
Slip 1ChangeOne slip kept for the top / outside edge.
Short legChangeCatcher in front of square for the fend or glove off the hip.
GullyChangeThe steer or fend that flies squarer of the wicket.
PointChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
CoverChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-offChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Mid-onChangeRing saver for the bumper plan.
Deep backward squareChangeDeep behind square for the top-edged pull.
Deep fine legChangeDeep behind square for the hook.
